extraction.one_line_summary: WELLENS Tim won from a seven-rider breakaway by taking the inside line through the final left-hand corner 100m out, holding off WOODS Michael's superior speed.
tactical_patterns: WELLENS pre-studied finish reconnaissance from hotel room, prioritizing last-corner positioning over raw speed advantage against faster finishers.
Breakaway formation required 60km to establish when approaching third week, indicating peloton fatigue enables elite riders to escape.
MARTIN Dan launched speculative acceleration on punchy finish despite unsuitable terrain for CARTHY Hugh, testing GC gaps opportunistically.
TERPSTRA Niki crashed in defeat zone after first KOM, confirming peloton middle is most dangerous positioning when pace drops post-climb.

rider_signals.WELLENS Tim: positive: demonstrated tactical maturity prioritizing corner positioning over wattage, suggests course study compensates for pure power deficits
rider_signals.WOODS Michael: negative: had superior speed but lost due to poor final corner positioning, indicates tactical preparation gap
rider_signals.CARTHY Hugh: negative: struggled on steep punchy finish unsuitable to his wheelhouse, confirms limited winning terrain profile
rider_signals.MARTIN Dan: neutral: tested GC riders on punchy finish but lacked sufficient gap, shows opportunistic attacking instinct remains
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: positive: maintained 39-second gap without effort on lumpy stage, confirms GC control depth heading to final mountain test
